task_completion.description: Open the raw mitochondria image in Fiji/ImageJ
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji/ImageJ interface is open and displaying the 'mitochondria_raw.tif' image.

task_completion.description: Preprocess and segment the image to isolate the mitochondrial network
task_completion.visual_evidence: A binary (black and white) or thresholded mask of the mitochondrial network is visible in the Fiji image window.

task_completion.description: Calculate shape descriptors (Aspect Ratio and Circularity) and save to shape_metrics.csv
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Results table showing shape measurements (specifically AR/Aspect Ratio and Circ/Circularity) is visible, along with evidence of saving it to '~/Fiji_Data/results/mitochondria/shape_metrics.csv' (e.g., a Save dialog or terminal command).

task_completion.description: Skeletonize the binary image for network topology analysis
task_completion.visual_evidence: A skeletonized version of the image (1-pixel wide lines representing the mitochondrial network structure) is visible in Fiji.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting skeleton metrics to skeleton_metrics.csv
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results window from the skeleton analysis (e.g., 'Analyze Skeleton' results showing branches, junctions, etc.) is visible and being saved to '~/Fiji_Data/results/mitochondria/skeleton_metrics.csv'.

task_completion.description: Save the labeled skeleton map visualization to skeleton_map.png
task_completion.visual_evidence: A labeled skeleton map (typically color-coded to show branches, junctions, and endpoints) is visible in a Fiji window and being saved to '~/Fiji_Data/results/mitochondria/skeleton_map.png'.

integrity.description: Agent used Fiji/ImageJ to perform the analysis rather than mocking the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Fiji/ImageJ GUI, including menus, thresholding tools, or the Analyze Skeleton plugin interface being actively used.

integrity.description: The segmented mask and skeleton map correspond to the actual input image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The structural patterns in the binary mask and the labeled skeleton map must visually match the biological structures seen in the original mitochondria_raw.tif image.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the image analysis by directly writing fake data to the CSV files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal commands (like 'echo' or simple python scripts) directly writing arbitrary numbers into shape_metrics.csv or skeleton_metrics.csv without extracting them from Fiji.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image is derived from Fiji's built-in HeLa Cells sample (Channel 2). The expected output files are: shape_metrics.csv, skeleton_metrics.csv, and skeleton_map.png in ~/Fiji_Data/results/mitochondria/. Note on success thresholds: The actual verifier requires the mean Aspect Ratio (AR) to be > 1.4 (not 1.5 as stated in metadata) and the number of branches to be > 5 (not 50). If you can read the CSV contents in the screenshots, use these verified thresholds (>1.4 AR, >5 branches) to assess correctness.
